_description: Anti-patterns are learnings from content that FAILED. High views + low conversion = wrong audience. High clicks + no signups = weak landing. Agents MUST check this before creating new content to avoid repeating mistakes.

rules.rule: If a hook gets >1000 views but <1% click-through, tag as wrong-audience and log the audience signal that attracted wrong viewers.
If a hook gets high clicks but <2% conversion to petition/donation, the CTA or landing experience is broken — fix the funnel before creating more hooks.
If an image post outperforms text-only by >2x engagement, shift 70% of content to image-first formats on that platform.
If controversy hooks (anti-university framing) get high engagement but drive negative sentiment and no conversions, BAN that framing permanently. Community-positive framing only.
If a Reddit post gets removed or downvoted to 0, log the subreddit's posting rules as an anti-pattern. Do NOT post similar content there again without rule compliance.
